evals.prompt: What SDLC runs do we have going right now?
evals.expected_output: Enumerates the run directories under docs/superpowers/runs/, newest first, with each run's current phase and status pulled from meta.json — no writes of any kind.

assertions.name: catalog-is-read-only
assertions.description: Listing every run produces no writes anywhere — this is pure R0 inspection.

assertions.name: newest-run-first
assertions.description: Runs are ordered with the most recently created run at the top of the list.

evals.prompt: Run PROJ-4471 stopped partway through — can you pick it back up?
evals.expected_output: Shows the run's current phase and status, then explicitly asks the user to confirm before handing the resume off to sdlc-pipeline — it does not resume unprompted.

assertions.name: resume-waits-for-yes
assertions.description: The skill surfaces the run's state and asks for explicit confirmation before any resume handoff happens.

assertions.name: delegates-execution-elsewhere
assertions.description: Once confirmed, the actual phase execution is handed to sdlc-pipeline rather than run by this skill itself.

evals.prompt: Resume the run that finished last week, PROJ-4200.
evals.expected_output: Checks meta.status first, finds it completed, and refuses to resume or write anything to that run's directory — a completed run is treated as immutable no matter what the user asks for.

assertions.name: completed-run-refuses-writes
assertions.description: A run whose meta.status is completed is never written to, and a resume request against it is declined.

evals.prompt: Resume PROJ-4310 — I know it was aborted earlier but we want to pick it up again.
evals.expected_output: Surfaces that the run's meta.status is aborted and asks a direct yes/no question before doing anything further; only proceeds to hand off a resume after an explicit yes.

assertions.name: aborted-run-needs-explicit-yes
assertions.description: An aborted run is never resumed on an implicit or assumed confirmation — the skill asks directly and waits.

evals.prompt: The status for PROJ-4390 looks off — meta.json says phase 4 but I thought it finished phase 6.
evals.expected_output: Reads events.jsonl, finds it shows phase 6 already completed, and treats the event log as the source of truth: appends a status.repaired event and corrects the meta.json snapshot to match — never rewrites or truncates events.jsonl itself.

assertions.name: event-log-wins-over-snapshot
assertions.description: When meta.json and events.jsonl disagree, the snapshot is corrected to match the append-only log, not the other way around.

assertions.name: correction-is-additive
assertions.description: Fixing the mismatch appends a status.repaired event rather than editing or truncating the existing event history.
